prompt:
You are a B2B prospecting analyst for a web agency. Analyze this locksmith's pricing transparency.

"Call for quote" is the norm in this industry — any pricing transparency is a competitive advantage.

HOT LEAD signals:
- No pricing information at all
- "Call for a quote" with no guidance
- No mention of callout charges
- No indication of price range

COLD LEAD signals:
- Starting-from prices for common jobs
- Callout charge clearly stated
- Price calculator or estimator
- "No hidden fees" guarantee
- Price comparison vs competitors
- Free quote form with structured inputs

Return each record as:
{
  "url": "<page url>",
  "pageName": "<short name>",
  "summary": "<1-2 sentence overview of pricing transparency>",
  "opportunityScore": "<1-10, higher = less transparent = better lead>",
  "pricingType": "<listed|ranges|call-only|none>",
  "hasCalloutCharge": true/false,
  "hasQuoteForm": true/false,
  "hasPriceGuarantee": true/false,
  "topOpportunity": "<what a web agency could sell here>",
  "notes": "<one sentence prospecting assessment>"
}

---
You are analyzing web pages for the category described above.
For each page provided below, extract one record.
Follow the JSON schema from the category description exactly.

## Required fields (always include)
- "url": the page URL
- "pageName": short human-readable page name
- "summary": 1-2 sentence plain-language overview

## Field naming conventions (follow strictly)
- Scores: name ends with "Score", value is a number 1-10 (e.g. "conversionScore": 7)
- Booleans: name starts with "has"/"is" or ends with "Visible" (e.g. "pricingVisible": true)
- Short text: "notes", "description" — 1-2 sentences max
- Tags/lists: arrays of short strings (e.g. "classStyles": ["Hatha", "Vinyasa"])
- Plain strings: everything else (e.g. "price": "€15 drop-in")

Return a JSON object: { "records": [<one record per page>] }.
No markdown, no code fences.
response: {"records":[{"url":"https://www.banham.co.uk/keyholding/pricing/","pageName":"Banham Keyholding Pricing","summary":"Banham provides a dedicated pricing page for their keyholding service, listing clear annual subscription tiers with specific pound amounts for different service levels. This is unusually transparent for the industry, with named packages and stated prices visible without needing to call.","opportunityScore":2,"pricingType":"listed","hasCalloutCharge":false,"hasQuoteForm":false,"hasPriceGuarantee":false,"topOpportunity":"Very limited — pricing is already well-presented; could potentially pitch UX improvements or a price comparison tool, but this is a strong lead for transparency not conversion issues.","notes":"Banham is a cold lead — their pricing page is well-structured and transparent, making them one of the least promising prospects for a web agency pitch around pricing visibility."}]}
